frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: gradual integration of sensory evidence toward a decision bound

frameworks.name: circuit causal-contribution framework
frameworks.one_line: regions must causally influence behavior, encode graded evidence, and contribute throughout accumulation

core_question: Which brain region directly implements gradual evidence accumulation during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: an accumulator region must causally affect choice behavior
an accumulator region should show graded neural encoding of accumulated evidence
such a region must contribute throughout the accumulation epoch, not only at onset or end
manipulations: pharmacological inactivation of anterior dorsal striatum (ADS)
optogenetic perturbation timed within the accumulation epoch
key_findings: ADS perturbation alters accumulation-based choice behavior
ADS neurons show graded encoding of accumulated evidence
ADS contributes throughout the accumulation process, not just at one phase
previously implicated cortical regions (PPC, frontal orienting fields) appear not to be directly involved
interpretations: ADS is the first identified brain region to satisfy all three criteria for direct involvement in evidence accumulation
ADS may be a primary node in the network responsible for accumulation
